source_inspiration.name: agency-orchestrator
source_inspiration.adaptation: Daedalus uses dependency-aware workflow definitions and execution levels, but keeps execution inside its existing Python/CrewAI/OpenClaw safety gates.

workflows.name: Feature Delivery Mesh
workflows.description: Plan, implement, validate, and release a scoped server feature.

steps.task: Define scope, success criteria, blockers, and human approval gates.
Convert the milestone brief into player-facing design and acceptance criteria.
Create implementation slices, dependencies, risk gates, and work-order sequence.
Plan zones, quest flow, NPC placement, and encounter pacing.
Review rates, rewards, shops, sinks, and abuse-prone progression.
Implement the smallest approved vertical slice through the guarded coding worker.
Assess client, interface, cache, and user-facing compatibility impact.
Run available build/test/smoke checks and record evidence.
Perform defensive review for dupes, permission issues, and unsafe trust boundaries.
Prepare release checklist, rollback plan, and go/no-go status.
Update docs, memory, and implementation handoff notes.
Verify branch health, cron readiness, logs, and optional GitHub push gates.

workflows.name: Visual Product Pass
workflows.description: Improve dashboard/client-facing presentation with design, implementation, QA, and docs gates.

steps.task: Define visual target, UX goals, and acceptance criteria.
Check asset provenance, visual consistency, and implementation readiness.
Map visual direction into concrete UI/client changes.
Apply the smallest coherent UI/client patch.
Run smoke checks and screenshot/interaction QA where available.
Update style notes and future-agent instructions.

workflows.name: Incident Recovery
workflows.description: Triage failed autonomous work and recover without repeating known mistakes.

steps.task: Collect branch, log, cron, and worker state.
Review whether the failure created unsafe state or permission risk.
Reproduce the failure with the smallest safe command and record evidence.
Create a minimal recovery work order and rollback path.
Update failed-attempt memory and operator handoff.

workflows.name: Profitability Review
workflows.description: Evaluate ethical server sustainability, costs, revenue streams, retention, and economy impact before monetization work.

steps.task: Define the sustainability question, target audience, constraints, and non-pay-to-win policy.
Assess whether proposed revenue streams distort rates, shops, trade, rewards, or progression.
Review monetization options for fairness, transparency, retention, and community trust.
Identify fraud, chargeback, market manipulation, account service, and duping risks.
Model downside/base/upside scenarios and identify data needed for confidence.
Define telemetry and validation checks for retention, conversion, and support load.
Prepare go/no-go recommendation with rollout, rollback, and monitoring plan.
Document assumptions, ethics gates, metrics, and next work orders.
